preparations.preparation_desc: Achillea millefolium is a medicinal herb commonly known as yarrow, used for centuries in traditional medicine to promote digestive health and reduce inflammation. The infusion made from its leaves and flowers is often consumed to ease menstrual cramps, fever, and cold symptoms, while also supporting the immune system. Additionally, yarrow infusion is also used topically to aid in wound healing and reduce swelling due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.

Achillea millefolium is a popular herbal remedy known for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, often used to create a tincture for topical application. The tincture, derived from the leaves and flowers of the plant, is commonly used to soothe wounds, reduce swelling, and promote healing. When used as directed, achillea millefolium tincture can be an effective and natural way to support the body's natural healing processes.

Achillea millefolium is a fragrant and versatile essential oil extracted from the flowers of the yarrow plant, known for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. This oil is often used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation, reduce stress and anxiety, and improve sleep quality. Additionally, it is also valued for its ability to support wound healing and reduce the appearance of scars and stretch marks.

Achillea millefolium is a traditional medicinal herb commonly used to create a poultice for various health issues, including reducing inflammation and soothing minor skin irritations. The poultice is made by mixing the dried leaves and flowers of the plant with a binding agent, such as oil or water, to create a paste that can be applied directly to the affected area. By applying the poultice, individuals have reported relief from symptoms associated with sprains, strains, and minor burns.
